project: GIS MVP - Girls In Sports Media Catalog & AI Marketing Composer
description: Next.js PWA on port 3010 that leverages Immich for media storage/distribution. AI-orchestrated marketing material creation using LLM planning + ffmpeg/media tools to compose collages and videos from existing camp media. Includes feedback loop via positive/negative example albums for future prompt improvement. Directors upload via Immich; GIS app handles catalog, selection, AI analysis, composition, and results.

userStories.title: Project Bootstrap — Next.js + Prisma + PostgreSQL on port 3010
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the GIS app scaffolded with the same Next.js/Prisma stack as DojoMojo so that I have a consistent, familiar foundation.
acceptanceCriteria: Next.js 14+ app initialized and running on port 3010
Prisma schema file exists with PostgreSQL datasource configured
Database connection succeeds on startup
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Implemented. Next.js 14.2.35, React 18, Prisma 7.8.0 with @prisma/adapter-pg, Tailwind 3.4.1. Schema includes Event, GeneratedAsset, FeedbackRating. PostgreSQL database girlsinsports synced. Build passes, tsc clean. Committed as 5428390.

userStories.title: Static Auth Gateway
userStories.description: As a director, I want a simple login gate so that the app is protected without complex auth for MVP, while the architecture supports upgrading to full auth later.
acceptanceCriteria: Login page exists with static username/password (env-configured)
Authenticated sessions persist via HTTP-only cookie
All routes behind the gateway redirect unauthenticated users to login
Auth middleware is modular — easily swappable for full OAuth/NextAuth later
Typecheck passes
Verify UI renders correctly in browser at expected route
userStories.notes: Implemented: login page, POST /api/auth/login/logout, JWT cookie sessions, middleware.ts route guards, dashboard placeholder. Committed as 6e1b5f5.

userStories.title: Immich Integration — Album & Asset Discovery
userStories.description: As a director, I want the GIS app to discover and display Immich albums and assets so that I can browse all camp media uploaded via the Immich mobile app.
acceptanceCriteria: Immich server URL and API key are configurable via environment variables
App fetches albums from Immich API and displays them in the GIS UI
Albums are mapped to GIS events via metadata (event name, sport, city, date)
App fetches asset thumbnails and metadata (file type, dimensions, upload date) from Immich
Typecheck passes
Verify UI renders correctly in browser at expected route
userStories.notes: Implemented: src/lib/immich.ts with albums/assets/thumbnails. API routes for albums and album detail. Thumbnail proxy. Committed as b08df59.

userStories.title: Event Catalog & Media Browser
userStories.description: As a director, I want to browse media by GIS event with filtering so that I can quickly find relevant photos and videos.
acceptanceCriteria: Event list page displays all GIS events with thumbnail previews
Event detail page shows all associated media in a responsive grid
Filters available: sport, city, date range, file type (image/video)
Media grid supports lazy loading for performance
Typecheck passes
Verify UI renders correctly in browser at expected route
userStories.notes: Implemented: /events/[id] page with header, description, responsive grid, All/Photos/Videos filter, lazy-loaded thumbnails. Committed as a1c5d67.

userStories.title: Media Selection & Output Type Selection
userStories.description: As a director, I want to select media and choose an output format so that I can request composed marketing materials.
acceptanceCriteria: Users can multi-select individual media assets with checkboxes
'Select All' toggle available for the current filtered view
After selection, user chooses output type: Collage Poster, 15s Highlight Video, or Full Wrap-up Video
Optional 'Let AI choose the best' toggle that passes all media to the ranking layer
Selection state persists while navigating within the event
Typecheck passes
Verify UI renders correctly in browser at expected route
userStories.notes: Implemented: selection mode with checkboxes, select/deselect all, count badge, Let AI choose checkbox, output type slide-up panel with 3 options. Committed as 18f8eed.

userStories.title: AI Layer 1 — Vision Analysis & Best Media Ranking via Venice.ai
userStories.description: As a director, I want the AI to analyze selected media and rank the best shots so that the final composition uses only high-quality, impactful content.
acceptanceCriteria: App sends selected media (or all, if 'AI choose best' is selected) to Venice.ai vision model
Vision prompt instructs the model to evaluate: composition, action/motion, faces visible, lighting, emotional impact, relevance to youth sports
Model returns ranked list of asset IDs with confidence scores and brief justification per asset
Top-ranked assets are selected for the composition layer
If manual selection was used, ranking acts as a secondary filter/reordering
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Implemented: src/lib/vision.ts model-agnostic layer. Evaluates composition (20), action (20), faces (20), lighting (15), relevance (15), technical (10). POST /api/ai/vision/rank. UI ranking modal with gold/silver/bronze badges. Fallback ranking when model unavailable. Committed as 37ef36d.

userStories.title: AI Layer 2 — Composition Script Generation via Venice.ai LLM
userStories.description: As a director, I want the AI to plan the composition (layout, order, cuts, transitions, captions) so that the final output tells a compelling story about the event.
acceptanceCriteria: App sends ranked media list + event metadata + output type to Venice.ai text model
LLM returns a structured composition script: for collages — grid layout, image positions, caption text; for videos — clip order, durations, transitions, music tempo suggestion
Script includes branded captions (event name, sport, city, date) styled appropriately
Script is validated against available media before execution
User can review and edit the generated script before execution
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Implemented: src/lib/composer.ts model-agnostic director. Typed CollageScript and VideoScript outputs with brand-aware colors. POST /api/ai/composition. UI review panel with human-readable summary and raw JSON. Committed as 86386e9.

userStories.title: Media Composition Engine — ffmpeg + Image Tools Execution
userStories.description: As a director, I want the app to execute the AI-planned composition using media tools so that I receive a polished final file.
acceptanceCriteria: Server-side composition engine accepts a composition script and source media files
For Collage Posters: uses image manipulation library (sharp/libvips or Pillow) to crop, resize, arrange images per script, overlay text/captions, apply branded color borders
For Videos: uses ffmpeg to concatenate clips, apply transitions, add text overlays, match durations to script, output MP4 at appropriate resolution
Composition runs asynchronously with progress tracking
Failed compositions surface readable error logs
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Implemented: src/lib/media-engine.ts with executeCollage() via sharp (canvas, composite, text overlay) and executeVideo() via ffmpeg (concat, zoompan, drawtext, branded outro). POST /api/composition/execute creates DB record, executes, updates status. Committed as 930f68c.

userStories.title: Results Catalog & Immich Upload
userStories.description: As a director, I want generated marketing materials saved to Immich and browsable in the app so that I can download them via the Immich mobile app.
acceptanceCriteria: Generated files are uploaded to a dedicated 'GIS Generated' album in Immich via server-side API
Generated assets are cataloged in the app's database with metadata: source event, output type, composition script used, generation date
Results page displays all generated materials with preview thumbnails from Immich
Each result includes: download link (via Immich), event info, output type, created date
Typecheck passes
Verify UI renders correctly in browser at expected route
userStories.notes: Implemented: GET /api/results, GET /api/results/[id], POST /api/results/[id]/upload. /results catalog page and /results/[id] detail. Uploads via multipart/form-data to Immich API, optionally adds to event album. Committed as bdde0f3.

userStories.title: Results Download & Preview
userStories.description: As a director, I want to preview and download generated files from the GIS app so that I can share them immediately.
acceptanceCriteria: Preview available for images (thumbnail) and videos (first frame + play indicator)
Download button streams the file from Immich to the user's browser
File downloads with descriptive filename including event and output type
Mobile users see a note: 'Also available in your Immich app for mobile download'
Typecheck passes
Verify UI renders correctly in browser at expected route
userStories.notes: Implemented: GET /api/results/[id]/download serves files with proper Content-Type and Content-Disposition. Inline preview: for images,

userStories.title: Feedback Loop — Positive & Negative Example Albums
userStories.description: As a director, after reviewing a generated result, I want to mark source media as 'good' or 'not suitable' so that the system learns and improves future AI selections.
acceptanceCriteria: After viewing a generated result, user can rate the source media used: thumbs-up (positive example) or thumbs-down (negative example)
Positive-rated media are copied to a hidden 'GIS Positive Examples' album in Immich
Negative-rated media are copied to a hidden 'GIS Negative Examples' album in Immich
Ratings are stored in the app's database linked to the media asset ID, event, generation job, and user feedback timestamp
Future vision analysis prompts can optionally reference positive/negative examples to improve ranking accuracy (feature-flagged for post-MVP)
Typecheck passes
Verify UI renders correctly in browser at expected route
userStories.notes: Implemented: POST /api/feedback stores POSITIVE/NEGATIVE ratings per sourceAssetId. MediaCard hover-reveals 👍/👎 buttons. Ratings persisted to FeedbackRating table. Loads existing feedback on page mount. Post-MVP: wire to vision prompt context. Committed as f034d19.

userStories.title: AI Assistant Chat for Composition Guidance
userStories.description: As a director, I want an AI chat assistant to help me craft better output requests and clarify options so that I get higher-quality marketing output.
acceptanceCriteria: Chat panel available on the generation page
User can ask the assistant for help with output type recommendations, composition ideas, or media selection tips
Assistant has context of the current event, selected media count, and available output types
Assistant responses are streamed via Venice.ai text model
Chat history is session-scoped (not persisted)
Typecheck passes
Verify UI renders correctly in browser at expected route
userStories.notes: Implemented: src/lib/chat.ts with model-agnostic chat completion. System prompt: GIS composition expert with brand guidelines. POST /api/chat with event context. Floating 🤖 FAB button opens slide-up panel with message history and input. Supports Enter to send. Committed as 5b39519.
